entry.title: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content:
Last week we had completed the system set up and also created a package with your community ID. We had also gone through the help document on SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities.

Now let us dive into the world of ABAP AI by creating an application where Joule will guide you during each step.

Quick Links

Please use this separate thread to ask your questions and discuss issues. 

Task 1: Create a ABAP RAP application using 'OData UI Service from Scratch’ wizard

It can be any application of your choice preferably non enterprise related to keep the challenge simple and more interesting. One field should be an amount field and another one a date field so that we can leverage J4D(Joule for Developers) to add some logic in the subsequent weeks. You should have 3 more fields which will be used to try out some of the J4D features.

Note: It is recommended to create all the fields while creating the app itself to avoid any manual coding later.

Example:- You can create a book store application consisting of fields ID, Title, Author, Price, Published Date, Stock, Discounted Price, BookAge. Here the last 3 fields can be used to try out the Joule for Developers (J4D) features. You are free to choose any dataset and logic combinations.

  1. Right click on the package ZAC_<your community ID> created and select ‘Generate ABAP Repository Objects’Generate ABAP Repository Objects’
  2. In the below popup that appears choose ‘Odata UI Service from Scratch’ and click on ‘Next’ two times.Odata UI Service from Scratch
  3. Click ‘Next’ again.Click Next
  4. In the next screen make sure you get the Joule Help to the right of Wizard popup. Here in the section ‘Ask Joule to update the fields’ can provide your prompts to create the application you like by specifying the fields and other details. Hit enter. Note: It is better to create all objects ending with your <community ID> to avoid any naming conflicts. Try to specify this in the prompt.Joule option in WizardNote: Make sure to select the option -  ‘Transactional with Draft’ highlighted in the above screenshot is selected to enable ‘Create’ option in the application getting created. For single entity sometimes it is set as ‘Without Draft’.
  5. Joule will now propose the Entity and Fields based on your prompt. Please note that we still should verify whether the suggestions are accurate for our requirements. Here you can notice that sometimes Joule suggests to change the Service Configuration to ‘Transactional Without Draft’, we can handle this in next step.Joule Field Proposal
  6. Once you are satisfied click on ‘Accept’ and you can see that the ‘Artifact Suffix’ if specified and the ‘Project Name’ gets added as shown in the below screenshot.           Here remember to change the Service Configuration to ‘Transactional with Draft’ and click ‘Next’.Joule Accept Proposal
  7. Joule lists the artifacts that will be created. Verify them again and click on ‘Next’ and then ‘Finish’ on the next screen. 
  8. Refresh the package and check all the artifacts have been created. Publish the Service Binding and preview the application.
  9. The application will open and should have a ‘Create’ button. Here you can either create a data generator class and add some simple logic to fill data / Use the ‘Create’ option in the app to create some sample records. ( Please create 4-5 entries in any case ). You can also adjust the metadata if you like.Application Created

For more information: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/use-odata-ui-service-from-scratch-wizard?locale=en-US

Task 2: Add a simple logic in the CDS data definition

  1. Add some simple logic in the CDS data definition for example to provide 2% discount on actual Price and fill another field say ‘DiscountedPrice’ with the discounted value for all records in the database.

    Note: The logic and examples are mentioned just for your reference and to make the purpose of the challenge easy to understand. Please feel free to use your own logic and dataset.

  2. Now that one of the fields was enhanced, you may need to recreate the draft table ending with _D### before launching the application again. If you do not face any issue you can skip this step.

    To do that, go to the Project Explorer and open the behavior definition click on the icon as shown on the picture to follow the quick fix. Save and activate the draft table.

    Recreate Draft Table

Task 3: Use Joule->Explain capability to understand code

Use ‘Explain’ capability of Joule to get detailed explanation of the code added.

Hint: Please add few lines of code like a simple CASE statement in CDS data definition to test Explain feature.

You may also watch this video to know more about how to use Joule->Explain feature.

We will be back next week with another feature of Joule AI Capability. Stay Tuned!!

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - データアナライザ
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能をつかってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、「ストーリーを見ている時にグラフやテーブルに表示されていない分析軸を含めたより詳細な情報や差異の要因を知りたい」際に分析軸や分析対象の数値項目を追加したり入れ替えたりしながらアドホック分析を行う機能である、データアナライザについてご紹介いたします。

 

目次：

  • データアナライザの概要
  • データアナライザの利用方法[ハンズオン]
    • データアナライザの有効化
    • データアナライザを利用した分析
    • インサイトをストーリーに固定：分析内容をストーリー上にピン留めしてすぐにアクセスできるように設定

※ベースとなるサンプルストーリーの作成⼿順は、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityでご紹介しています。
※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画⾯キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年3⽉に作成しています。）

データアナライザの概要

SAP Analytics Cloudのデータアナライザ機能は、分析軸や分析対象の数値項目を追加したり入れ替えたりしながらアドホック分析を行うことで、分析を深めたい部分に関してのチャートやテーブルを追加で作成することなく、ユーザーが即座にアドホックに詳細分析を行うことに役立ちます。

詳しくはHelp Portalもご参照ください：データアナライザ

※データアナライザはストーリーからだけでなく、機能メニューやファイルリポジトリ、JustAsk機能などから利用することも可能です。本ブログではストーリーからの起動についてご紹介します。詳細はHelp Portalをご確認ください： データアナライザの起動

データアナライザの利用方法

0. はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityで作成したストーリーを編集モードで開こう！

0.1. ストーリーのナビゲーションバーから「編集」をクリックします。

 

1. データアナライザを有効化しよう！

1.1. 対象のチャートまたはテーブルをクリックし、右パネル（ビルダパネル）を開きます。

 

1.2 パネルメニューをスクロールダウンし、「チャートのプロパティ」＞「表示モード」から「データアナライザを有効化」にチェックをつけます。

※チャートやテーブルからのデータアナライザの利用設定はストーリー設定から一括で有効化することも可能です。一括で有効化したい場合には「ストーリー設定」＞「ストーリー詳細」＞「データ探索の設定」を有効化します。

 

1.3. ストーリーを保存し、ストーリーを「表示モード」に切り替えます。

 

2. データアナライザを使って分析しよう！

2.1. 詳細に分析したいチャートまたはテーブルをクリックし、メニューから「データアナライザを開く」を選択します。（「現在のタブで開く」「新規タブで開く」は任意に選択ください）

 

2.2. データアナライザが開かれます。画面には2.1で選択したチャートのデータが表示されています。メニューバーのフィルタボタンを押すと、ストーリーのチャートで利用されていたフィルタが引き継がれていることが確認できます。

 2.3. 画面右の「≪（展開）」ボタンをクリックし、ビルダパネルを開きます。

 

2.4. 追加したい項目を「利用可能なオブジェクト」から行または列のアイコンをクリックするかドラッグ＆ドロップで「ビルダ」に追加し、ディメンションやメジャーを追加します。（今回は例として「製品グループ」を行に追加します）

 2.5. 2.4の操作を繰り返しながら分析を行います。行に配置された項目を列にドラッグ＆ドロップで移動したり、項目の並び順を入れ替えることも可能です。また、「表示タイプ」からチャートでの表示に切り替えることも可能です。

操作についての 詳細はHelp Portalもご参照ください：データアナライザ

 

3. データアナライザの分析内容をストーリー上にピン留めしてすぐにアクセスできるように設定

をしよう！

データアナライザのインサイトをストーリーに固定することでデータアナライザの分析内容をストーリー上にピン留めしてすぐにアクセスできるように設定することが可能です。作成したインサイトに直接アクセスすることができるため分析を効率化することができます。

3.1. メニューバーから「保存」を選択します。保存ダイアログで、インサイトの名前を追加し、「保存してストーリーに固定」を選択します。※ストーリーの「表示」モードからデータアナライザを起動する必要があります。

 

3.2 ストーリーに戻り、チャートまたはテーブルをクリックし、メニューから「データアナライザを開く」を選択します。固定されたインサイトが表示されていることが確認できます。固定されたインサイトを選択し、3.1で保存したインサイトに直接アクセスできます。

 

今回はデータアナライザ 機能のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: ZUGFERD vs XRECHNUNG-B2B Customer Invoices from External System
entry.content: Good question — this comes up often in hybrid invoice distribution setups. For ZUGFeRD via email, the middleware does NOT need to transform to CII before sending to SAP DRC. Here is how the correct flow works: SAP DRC handles the format conversion internally. Your middleware sends the invoice data to SAP DRC in UBL 2.1 (same as your Peppol flow). SAP DRC then: 1. Creates the eDocument from the UBL 2.1 input 2. Converts internally to CII (Cross Industry Invoice) format — which is the XML standard embedded in ZUGFeRD PDF/A-3 3. Generates the hybrid ZUGFeRD PDF (PDF/A-3 with embedded CII XML) 4. Delivers via email through eDoc_cockpit So your middleware only needs ONE output format — UBL 2.1 — for both Peppol and ZUGFeRD customers. SAP DRC handles the CII conversion for ZUGFeRD. What to watch for in this setup: · ZUGFeRD profile level — confirm your client needs COMFORT or EXTENDED level, not just MINIMUM. The profile level determines which fields are mandatory in the CII output. · PDF/A-3 compliance — the PDF container must be PDF/A-3 (not regular PDF/A-1 or PDF/A-2) for the XML attachment to be ZUGFeRD compliant. · DRC output channel config — make sure the eDocument output channel for email customers is set to ZUGFeRD and not XRechnung profile in DRC config. One thing to validate before go-live: the CII XML embedded in your ZUGFeRD PDF should be validated against EN16931 schematron rules — not just the PDF structure. We built Readix (readix.evidra.online) specifically for this pre-flight validation — checks the invoice data against ZUGFeRD, XRechnung, KSeF and ebInterface requirements before go-live. First check is free if useful for your client project.

entry.title: Re: Regenerate Outgoing Payment File
entry.content:
Good day @ChinYeeChia

Thank you for your question.

Question: How can I re-trigger the generation of the payment medium file for this specific, completed payment run to reflect the corrected data?

Answer: You normally cannot re generate the payment medium file for the same completed payment run just by correcting the bank name in the master data. Therefore, to have the bank name reflected in the payment file, the usual approach is:

  • Check whether the run is in an exceptional or cancelled state and use Delete Output only in that case, since SAP limits this reset option to exceptional runs, or
  • If the payment run is already completed, reverse the payment run (Manage Payment Media) or payment documents and then create a new payment run, so the system reads the corrected bank master data and generates accordingly a new payment medium.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/45698054f87c033de10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/031c345485b84c8c94265be9ef61d3a8/0bf4918dd83a4e92bc87fc575166e93f.html?locale=en-US

Here are steps I recommend to manage the payment process:

  • Open Manage Automatic Payments and verify the original run status.
  • Then in Manage Payment Media, check whether a file exists for that run.
  • Ensure you confirm the corrected bank name in the relevant bank master record.
  • If the run is completed, then reverse it and create a new payment run.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Support Required for Cancel Picking Process in EWM Public Cloud
entry.content:
We are facing an issue while cancelling the picking process in EWM.

Scenario Details:
Outbound Delivery Order Status:
• Picking Status – Completed
• Goods Issue Status – Not Started

The Warehouse Task was already confirmed and the stock moved to the staging storage type.
As per the standard process in private EWM, we usually use transaction /SCWM/CANCPICK to release the stock and create a new Warehouse Task to put the stock back to the source bin (either in background or foreground based on the putaway strategy).

However, since we are using S/4HANA Public Cloud, the transaction /SCWM/CANCPICK is not available.

Could you please advise the correct standard process in Public Cloud to:

  1. Release the picked stock from the outbound delivery.
  2. Create a putback warehouse task to move stock from staging area (S970) back to the original bin.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Enhancing Cost Center Security with Company Code Restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Security and access control are fundamental pillars of enterprise resource planning systems. As organizations grow and their data becomes increasingly sensitive, the need for granular authorization controls becomes paramount. SAP S/4HANA Cloud continues to evolve its authorization framework to meet these critical business requirements. For example: Authorization on company code level for app Manage Cost Centers F1443A

One significant enhancement to the authorization architecture is the introduction of Company Code restrictions based on company code. This new feature represents an important step forward in helping organizations maintain tighter control over who can access and manage cost center information across different business entities.

In this blog post, we explore this authorization enhancement, its implications for system administrators, and the steps necessary to ensure a smooth transition when this feature becomes active in upcoming versions(2608) of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Scenario: Understanding the Business Need

The Challenge of Cost Center Access Control

Cost centers are critical organizational structures used for allocating costs, tracking expenses, and managing financial reporting across different departments and business units. In large organizations with multiple company codes (representing different legal entities or business divisions), the challenge of managing who can access which cost centers becomes increasingly complex.

Consider this real-world scenario:

A global manufacturing company operates multiple subsidiaries, each with its own company code:

- Company Code 1000 (Europe Division)

- Company Code 2000 (Asia Division)

- Company Code 3000 (Americas Division)

Each division has its own cost centers and financial managers. Previously, without granular Company Code restrictions, a user authorized for cost center management might inadvertently access cost center data across all company codes—potentially exposing sensitive financial information and creating compliance risks.

The Solution: Company Code Restrictions

The new Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code feature addresses this security gap by enabling administrators to:

  1. Limit Cost Center Visibility: Restrict users to view only cost centers associated with their authorized company codes
  2. Control Modification Rights: Ensure that users can only modify cost center master data within their assigned company codes
  3. Enforce Compliance: Meet regulatory requirements for data segregation and access controls
  4. Improve Audit Trails: Create clear, auditable records of who accessed which cost centers

Implementation Details

 1.Authorization Requirements

To configure and manage Company Code restrictions, administrators must have specific authorization:

Required Business Catalog:

- `SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM` – This business catalog must be assigned to administrators responsible for identity and access management (IAM)

This ensures that only qualified IAM professionals can modify restriction configurations and prevent unauthorized changes to security settings.

 

2.Configuration Steps

Administrators must take proactive steps to configure the Company Code restriction before it becomes mandatory. Here's how:

Key Configuration Tool:

- App: Maintain Business Roles (F1492)

- Action: Preconfigure Company Code restriction values for all business roles that manage cost center master data

Configuration Process:

  1. Open the Maintain Business Roles app (F1492)
  2. Select the business role requiring Company Code restrictions
  3. Navigate to the restriction configuration section
  4. Assign appropriate company code values to the restriction
  5. Save and activate the configuration

Best Practice: Conduct this configuration during a low-usage period and test thoroughly in a sandbox environment before applying to production.

 

3. Affected Technical Objects

The Company Code restriction will impact access control across multiple layers of the SAP system:

CDS Views (Core Data Services):

- `I_CostCenter` – Main cost center master data view

- `I_CostCenterVH` – Cost center value help view

- `I_CostCenterSTDVH` – Cost center standard value help

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – Cost center transactional posting view

Data Dictionary Value Help:

- `KOST` – Traditional value help for cost centers

Business Object Interface:

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – Business object handling cost center transactions

 

4.Expected Changes in System Behavior

Once the Company Code restriction takes effect, the following changes will occur:

Access Restrictions:

- Users without proper Company Code authorization will no longer be able to view cost center master data outside their assigned company codes

- Attempts to access unauthorized cost centers will result in data being unavailable

- System actions involving cost center selection will filter options based on Company Code authorization

Impact on User Experience:

- Value help dropdowns (F4 help) will display only authorized cost centers

- Reports and analytics will show only data relevant to assigned company codes

- Cost center searches will return filtered results

Administrative Impact:

- Administrators must ensure all affected business roles have Company Code restrictions configured

- Failure to configure restrictions may result in users losing access to previously available data

 Configuration Timeline and Recommendations

 Phase 1: Immediate Action (Current)

- Review current authorization setup for cost center management

- Identify all business roles that handle cost center master data

- Audit current user access patterns to establish baseline data

 Phase 2: Pre-configuration (Before Upgrade)

- Configure Company Code restrictions using the Maintain Business Roles app (F1492)

- Assign appropriate company code values to each business role

- Test configuration in development/sandbox environment

 Phase 3: Validation and Testing

- Conduct end-to-end testing with representative users from each company code

- Verify that users can access only authorized cost centers

- Test value help, reports, and transaction access

- Document any access issues or unexpected behaviors

 Phase 4: Deployment (Upon Release)

- Deploy the update to production systems

- Monitor system performance and user feedback

- Address any access issues promptly

- Maintain documentation of changes

For additional information about this enhancement:

- SAP Note 3632368 - Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code - SAP for Me

- SAP Documentation: Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code | SAP Help Portal

 Conclusion

The introduction of Company Code restrictions in cost center master data management represents a meaningful advancement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud's authorization capabilities. By enabling organizations to enforce granular access controls based on company code assignments, this feature enhances security, improves compliance posture, and reduces the risk of unintended data exposure.

Key Takeaways:

  1. Proactive action is Essential: Don't wait until the feature is mandatory to configure Company Code restrictions. Preconfigure values now using the Maintain Business Roles app.
  2. Comprehensive Planning Required: Audit your current authorization structure, identify affected business roles, and plan your configuration strategy in advance.
  3. Testing is Critical: Thoroughly test the configuration in non-production environments before deploying to production to prevent user access issues.
  4. Administrative Oversight: Ensure that administrators with appropriate authorization (SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM) manage these configurations to maintain security.
  5. Ongoing Compliance: Regularly review and maintain Company Code restrictions as organizational structures and user roles evolve.

For organizations committed to maintaining robust security controls and regulatory compliance, this enhancement aligns perfectly with best practices in identity and access management. By taking a structured, well-planned approach to implementation, administrators can ensure a seamless transition when the Company Code restriction becomes mandatory in upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud versions.

The time to act is now. Begin your pre-configuration efforts today to ensure business continuity and security when this critical authorization enhancement takes effect.

Next Steps:

- Schedule a review of your current cost center authorization structure

- Identify business roles requiring Company Code restriction configuration

- Plan your implementation timeline to align with upcoming system updates

- Contact your SAP system administrators to begin pre-configuration efforts

entry.title: In /SCWM/BINMAT Field RMMQTY_FIX - What is the 'FIXED' checkmark for?
entry.content:
Asking the dumb question. 

What does the Fixed Tickbox (field RMMQTY_FIX) on transaction /SCWM/BINMAT actually do/impact/change?

For this case, Max 12 to a location. Minimum 4. Location contains 12.
Order picks 9 from the location. 
System generates a replenishment for 9 in either case with 'FIXED' ticked or not. 

The F1 - PA  shows the Header 'Minimum/Maximum/Replenishment Quantity Fixed' but no actual description or understanding of what it is used for.

entry.title: Re: Custom Domain Question in SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content: Hi @VamsiS, perhaps you already knew the answer. But anyway, after configuring custom domain in IAS, existing SAP domains continue to work. To make use of the IAS custom domain, from SAP BTP subaccount >> Security >> Trust Configuration, select OpenID Connect trust, check on Edit button and then change Domain to the custom IAS domain.

entry.title: Transferring Cost Centers in a Service Organization
entry.content:
This is about the service organization of a project.
Due to organizational changes,
I would like to move the cost center linked to the service organization to another service organization.
To do this, I need to delete the target cost center from the original service organization and assign it to the new service organization.
However, if the project period is still ongoing, moving the cost center to the new service organization will result in an error when saving the project changes, as the service organization and cost center are not linked.
Please tell me how SAP standards should be changed when changing a service organization due to an organizational change.

entry.title: Expose Joule Studio Agent/Skills as API for UI-triggered intelligent insights (without chatbot inter
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently exploring SAP Joule Studio capabilities and wanted to understand if it’s possible to expose Joule Studio agents or skills as an API/service so they can be triggered programmatically from a UI rather than through the Joule chatbot interface.

Use Case:
In our application UI, users interact with specific components (for example, clicking a marker or icon on a screen). When the user clicks this marker, we want to:

  1. Immediately trigger a backend call.

  2. Invoke a Joule Studio Agent or Skill that generates intelligent insights based on the context/data of that UI component.

  3. Return the generated insight and display it directly in a UI card or insight panel.

So instead of the user asking a question through the chatbot prompt, the insight is automatically generated and displayed on the UI based on the user action.

What I want to confirm:

  • Is it possible to expose Joule Studio agents/skills as an API endpoint that can be invoked programmatically?

  • Can Joule-generated insights be returned as structured output suitable for embedding in a custom UI component/card?

  • Are there recommended patterns or SAP services (e.g., AI Core, AI Launchpad, CAP services, etc.) that should be used to orchestrate this?

  • If Joule Studio itself cannot be directly exposed as an API, what would be the recommended architecture to achieve this UI-driven intelligent insight pattern?

The goal is to enable context-aware insights triggered directly from UI interactions, rather than requiring users to manually prompt a chatbot.

Any guidance, architecture suggestions, or documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: IoT: Air Quality Dashboard with SAP RAP and Mobile App
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

Once XSUAA instance is created, the respective XSUAA roles should be assigned to the technical user in the SAP BTP.

To connect to an SAP BTP OData service and send data, we first obtain a user token. With this token, we then request a CSRF token, which is used to securely send sensor data to the database.

CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) is a security mechanism that protects the backend from unauthorized data modifications. It ensures that write operations, such as POST or PUT requests, are performed only by trusted clients.

With the CSRF token, we can send requests to the OData service.

"

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: End-to-End SAP GUI Logon Automation with Codex and Python: Customizing + Reporting
entry.content:
Introduction

Many SAP users still perform repetitive tasks in SAP GUI manually — logging into systems, navigating transactions, adjusting customizing parameters, or extracting reports.With the rise of AI-assisted development and automation frameworks, it is now possible to orchestrate these operations programmatically.

In this article, I present an end-to-end automation scenario that combines:

  • Python
  • SAP GUI Scripting
  • AI-assisted coding with Codex

 

The solution demonstrates how an AI agent can:

  • Log into SAP GUI automatically
  • Execute customizing steps
  • Navigate transactions
  • Extract data and generate reports

By integrating AI-assisted scripting with SAP GUI automation, organizations can significantly accelerate operational tasks, reduce human error, and improve productivity.

This approach illustrates how AI agents can augment SAP consultants and administrators, enabling faster execution of routine activities and creating a foundation for more advanced intelligent automation in SAP landscapes.


Accelerate with AI Agents: Build, Adapt, and Report in SAP at Speed
 

 

1.What You Can Automate


First of all, I would like to thank you for inspiring me on this topic @Hrb24 
Claude Code + Python + SAP GUI Scripting: Your AI Agent for Any SAP Transaction 

With Codex + SAP GUI scripting, you can automate both daily operations and advanced SAP configuration work in a practical, scalable way:

  • Perform SAP customizing tasks directly from scripts
  • Execute mass customizing by providing Excel-based input files to Codex and applying changes in bulk
  • Run operational and analytical reports automatically (for example via SE16N-driven workflows)
  • Validate existing configurations and compare system state against expected setup rules
  • Build guided simulations for training and decision support (for example, simulate and identify the best price scenario for a specific material)

In short, you can move from manual, screen-by-screen execution to controlled, repeatable, and auditable automation across reporting, configuration, validation, and simulation use cases.

Prerequisites (Before Any Connection)

2.1 SAP-side prerequisites

  1. SAP GUI for Windows must be installed
  2. SAP Logon entry must exist and be tested manually
  3. SAP GUI Scripting must be enabled:
    • SAP GUI → Options → Accessibility & Scripting → Scripting
    • Enable scripting (client-side)
  4. Server-side scripting parameter must allow scripting:
    • sapgui/user_scripting = TRUE (Basis side)
  5. Your user needs authorization for:
    • target transactions (SE16N, OVX4, OVX5, etc.)
    • customizing save + transport assignment (if doing config)

2.2 Local environment prerequisites

  1. Python 3.10+ installed
  2. Install required packages:
    python -m pip install pywin32 openpyxl pypdf
  3. Keep SAP Logon open before script execution (recommended)
  4. Codex installed ( paid subscription required )

3. Project Structure (Recommended)

Use a simple folder structure:

automation/ sap_connect.py 
  • sap_connect.py: connection/bootstrap layer

4. Connection Script Design (Core)

Your sap_connect.py should handle:

  1. Attach to running SAP GUI:
    • GetObject("SAPGUI")
  2. Start SAP Logon if not running
  3. Open connection by exact SAP Logon entry name
  4. Wait until session window is ready
  5. Detect login screen vs already authenticated menu
  6. Handle post-login popups (information/multiple logon)
  7. Verify status bar for login errors
  8. Return a reusable session wrapper
    Example Script:

"""Open or create an SAP GUI session and authenticate (SSO or password).


"""

import argparse
import getpass
import logging
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

import win32com.client

try:
    from sap_scripting import SapSession
except Exception:
    class SapSession:
        def __init__(self):
            self.connection_index = 0
            self.session_index = 0
            self.application = None
            self.connection = None
            self.session = None

        def get_session_info(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
            info = self.session.Info
            return {
                "system": getattr(info, "SystemName", ""),
                "client": getattr(info, "Client", ""),
                "user": getattr(info, "User", ""),
                "transaction": getattr(info, "Transaction", ""),
                "response_time": getattr(info, "ResponseTime", ""),
            }


logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
)
log = logging.getLogger("sap_connect")


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SapConnectConfig:
    default_system: str = os.getenv("SAP_SYSTEM", "")
    default_client: str = os.getenv("SAP_CLIENT", "")
    default_user: str = os.getenv("SAP_USER", "")
    default_language: str = os.getenv("SAP_LANGUAGE", "EN")
    default_sso: bool = os.getenv("SAP_SSO", "true").lower() in ("1", "true", "yes")
    login_wait: float = 2.0
    popup_wait: float = 0.5
    startup_timeout: int = 30
    session_ready_timeout: float = 30.0
    saplogon_candidates: tuple = (
        r"C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\FrontEnd\SAPgui\saplogon.exe",
        r"C:\Program Files\SAP\FrontEnd\SAPgui\saplogon.exe",
        r"C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SAPLogon\saplogon.exe",
    )


CONFIG = SapConnectConfig()

DEFAULT_SYSTEM = CONFIG.default_system
DEFAULT_CLIENT = CONFIG.default_client
DEFAULT_USER = CONFIG.default_user
DEFAULT_LANGUAGE = CONFIG.default_language
DEFAULT_SSO = CONFIG.default_sso

LOGIN_SCREEN_FIELDS = (
    "wnd[0]/usr/txtRSYST-MANDT",
    "wnd[0]/usr/txtRSYST-BNAME",
)


def _mask(text: str, enabled: bool) -> str:
    if not enabled:
        return text
    if not text:
        return text
    if len(text) <= 2:
        return "*" * len(text)
    return text[0] + ("*" * (len(text) - 2)) + text[-1]


def _find_element(session: Any, element_id: str) -> Optional[Any]:
    try:
        return session.findById(element_id)
    except Exception:
        return None


def _attach_scripting_engine() -> Any:
    rot_entry = win32com.client.GetObject("SAPGUI")
    return rot_entry.GetScriptingEngine


def _ensure_saplogon_running() -> Any:
    try:
        app = _attach_scripting_engine()
        log.info("SAP Logon already running. Active connections: %s", app.Children.Count)
        return app
    except Exception:
        log.info("SAP Logon not detected - attempting to start it ...")

    exe_path = next((p for p in CONFIG.saplogon_candidates if os.path.isfile(p)), None)
    if exe_path is None:
        raise ConnectionError(
            "SAP Logon is not running and saplogon.exe was not found in:\n"
            + "\n".join(f"  {p}" for p in CONFIG.saplogon_candidates)
        )

    log.info("Launching SAP Logon from: %s", exe_path)
    subprocess.Popen([exe_path])

    deadline = time.time() + CONFIG.startup_timeout
    while time.time() < deadline:
        try:
            app = _attach_scripting_engine()
            log.info("SAP Logon started successfully.")
            return app
        except Exception:
            time.sleep(1.0)

    raise ConnectionError(
        f"SAP Logon did not become ready within {CONFIG.startup_timeout} seconds. "
        "Check SAP GUI scripting settings."
    )


def _wait_for_session_ready(connection: Any) -> Any:
    deadline = time.time() + CONFIG.session_ready_timeout
    while time.time() < deadline:
        try:
            session = connection.Children(0)
            _ = session.findById("wnd[0]").Text
            return session
        except Exception:
            time.sleep(0.5)
    raise RuntimeError("SAP window did not become ready within timeout after OpenConnection().")


def _detect_screen_state(session: Any) -> str:
    if _find_element(session, LOGIN_SCREEN_FIELDS[0]) is not None:
        return "LOGIN"

    try:
        tcode = session.Info.Transaction.strip()
        if tcode and tcode != "LOGIN":
            return "MENU"
    except Exception:
        pass

    try:
        if session.findById("wnd[0]").Text.strip():
            return "MENU"
    except Exception:
        pass

    return "UNKNOWN"


def _do_login(
    session: Any,
    client: str,
    user: str,
    password: str,
    language: str,
    sso: bool,
) -> None:
    mandt = _find_element(session, "wnd[0]/usr/txtRSYST-MANDT")
    if mandt and mandt.Changeable and client:
        mandt.text = client
        log.info("Client set.")

    if not sso:
        bname = _find_element(session, "wnd[0]/usr/txtRSYST-BNAME")
        bcode = _find_element(session, "wnd[0]/usr/pwdRSYST-BCODE")
        langu = _find_element(session, "wnd[0]/usr/txtRSYST-LANGU")

        if bname:
            bname.text = user
        if bcode:
            bcode.text = password
        if langu and langu.Changeable:
            langu.text = language

        log.info("Credentials filled (user/password mode).")
    else:
        log.info("SSO mode - skipping username/password fields")

    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0)
    log.info("Login submitted (Enter)")


def _handle_multiple_logon_popup(session: Any) -> None:
    log.info("Multiple logon detected - selecting OPT2 (keep existing sessions).")
    opt2 = _find_element(session, "wnd[1]/usr/radMULTI_LOGON_OPT2")
    if opt2:
        opt2.select()
    confirm = _find_element(session, "wnd[1]/tbar[0]/btn[0]")
    if confirm:
        confirm.press()
    else:
        session.findById("wnd[1]").sendVKey(0)


def _handle_post_login_popups(session: Any) -> None:
    time.sleep(CONFIG.popup_wait)
    for _ in range(5):
        popup = _find_element(session, "wnd[1]")
        if popup is None:
            break

        log.info("Popup detected: '%s'", popup.Text.strip())
        try:
            if _find_element(session, "wnd[1]/usr/radMULTI_LOGON_OPT1"):
                _handle_multiple_logon_popup(session)
            else:
                popup.sendVKey(0)
        except Exception as exc:
            log.warning("Popup handling failed: %s", exc)
        time.sleep(CONFIG.popup_wait)


def _verify_login(session: Any) -> None:
    sbar = _find_element(session, "wnd[0]/sbar")
    if not sbar:
        return

    msg_type = getattr(sbar, "MessageType", "")
    msg_text = getattr(sbar, "Text", "")
    if msg_type in ("E", "A"):
        raise RuntimeError(f"Login failed [{msg_type}]: {msg_text}")
    if msg_type == "W":
        log.warning("Login warning [%s]: %s", msg_type, msg_text)
    elif msg_type == "S" and msg_text:
        log.info("Login status [%s]: %s", msg_type, msg_text)


def _wrap_existing_session(application: Any, conn_idx: int, session_index: int = 0) -> SapSession:
    sap = object.__new__(SapSession)
    sap.connection_index = conn_idx
    sap.session_index = session_index
    sap.application = application
    sap.connection = application.Children(conn_idx)
    sap.session = sap.connection.Children(session_index)
    return sap


def connect_to_system(
    system: str = DEFAULT_SYSTEM,
    client: str = DEFAULT_CLIENT,
    user: str = DEFAULT_USER,
    password: str = "",
    language: str = DEFAULT_LANGUAGE,
    sso: bool = DEFAULT_SSO,
) -> SapSession:
    if not sso:
        if not user:
            user = input(f"SAP user for {system}/{client}: ").strip()
        if not password:
            password = getpass.getpass(f"Password for {user}@{system}: ")

    app = _ensure_saplogon_running()
    log.info("SAP Logon ready. Active connections before: %s", app.Children.Count)
    log.info("Opening connection to '%s' (SSO=%s) ...", system, sso)

    try:
        connection = app.OpenConnection(system, True)
    except Exception as exc:
        raise ConnectionError(
            f"Cannot open connection to '{system}': {exc}\n"
            "Check SAP Logon entry name (case-sensitive)."
        ) from exc

    session = _wait_for_session_ready(connection)
    log.info("Session opened.")

    state = _detect_screen_state(session)
    log.info("Screen state after OpenConnection: %s", state)
    if state == "LOGIN":
        _do_login(session, client, user, password, language, sso)
        time.sleep(CONFIG.login_wait)
    elif state == "MENU":
        log.info("SSO authenticated automatically - skipped login screen.")
    else:
        log.warning("Unexpected screen state '%s' - attempting to continue.", state)

    _handle_post_login_popups(session)
    _verify_login(session)

    conn_idx = app.Children.Count - 1
    sap = _wrap_existing_session(app, conn_idx, session_index=0)
    info = sap.get_session_info()
    log.info(
        "Connected - system=%s, client=%s, user=%s, transaction=%s",
        info["system"],
        info["client"],
        _mask(info["user"], True),
        info["transaction"],
    )
    return sap


def list_logon_entries(mask_sensitive: bool = True) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    try:
        app = _attach_scripting_engine()
        active: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        for i in range(app.Children.Count):
            conn = app.Children(i)
            for j in range(conn.Children.Count):
                sess = conn.Children(j)
                active.append(
                    {
                        "conn_idx": i,
                        "sess_idx": j,
                        "system": sess.Info.SystemName,
                        "client": _mask(sess.Info.Client, mask_sensitive),
                        "user": _mask(sess.Info.User, mask_sensitive),
                        "tcode": sess.Info.Transaction,
                    }
                )
        return active
    except Exception as exc:
        log.warning("list_logon_entries failed: %s", exc)
        return []


def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Open a new SAP session from SAP Logon and log in.",
        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
        epilog=(
            "Examples:\n"
            "  python sap_connect.py\n"
            "  python sap_connect.py --system MYSYS --client 100\n"
            "  python sap_connect.py --no-sso --user YOUR_USER\n"
            "  python sap_connect.py --list\n"
            "  python sap_connect.py --list --no-mask"
        ),
    )
    parser.add_argument("--system", default=DEFAULT_SYSTEM, help="SAP Logon entry description")
    parser.add_argument("--client", default=DEFAULT_CLIENT, help="SAP client number")
    parser.add_argument("--user", default=DEFAULT_USER, help="SAP username (ignored for SSO)")
    parser.add_argument("--language", default=DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, help="Logon language")
    parser.add_argument("--no-sso", dest="sso", action="store_false", help="Use password login")
    parser.set_defaults(sso=DEFAULT_SSO)
    parser.add_argument("--list", action="store_true", help="List active SAP sessions and exit")
    parser.add_argument("--no-mask", action="store_true", help="Do not mask user/client in output")
    return parser


def main() -> None:
    args = _build_parser().parse_args()

    if args.list:
        sessions = list_logon_entries(mask_sensitive=not args.no_mask)
        if not sessions:
            print("No active SAP sessions found.")
            return
        print(f"\nActive SAP sessions ({len(sessions)}):")
        for s in sessions:
            print(
                f"  [{s['conn_idx']}:{s['sess_idx']}]  "
                f"{s['system']}/{s['client']}  user={s['user']}  tcode={s['tcode']}"
            )
        return

    mode = "SSO" if args.sso else f"password (user={args.user or 'prompt'})"
    print(f"\nConnecting to {args.system} / client {_mask(args.client, not args.no_mask)}  [{mode}] ...")

    try:
        sap = connect_to_system(
            system=args.system,
            client=args.client,
            user=args.user,
            language=args.language,
            sso=args.sso,
        )
        info = sap.get_session_info()
        print(f"\n[OK] Connected: {info['system']} / {_mask(info['client'], not args.no_mask)} / {_mask(info['user'], not args.no_mask)}")
        print(f"  Transaction : {info['transaction']}")
        print(f"  Server      : {info['response_time']} ms response")
    except (ConnectionError, RuntimeError) as exc:
        log.error("%s", exc)
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
​



5. How to Connect (Execution)

5.1 List active sessions

python sap_connect.py --list

5.2 Connect with SSO

python sap_connect.py --system "Your SAP Logon Entry Name" --client 100

5.3 Connect with username/password

python sap_connect.py --system "Your SAP Logon Entry Name" --client 10

 

Important: --system must match SAP Logon entry description exactly (case-sensitive in many setups).

6. After Connection: Reporting Flow (SE16N)

A standard reporting script should do this:

  1. StartTransaction("SE16N")
  2. Set table (for example VBAK / VBRK)
  3. Set filters in selection fields (date/material/org/etc.)
  4. Execute (F8)
  5. Read ALV grid rows/columns
  6. Save output to Excel

Example use cases

  • Last 6 months sales report
  • Open request list (E070)
  • Material-level pricing checks

7. Excel Output Best Practices

For business users, generate:

  • Sheet 1: summary table (monthly totals/KPIs)
  • Sheet 2: raw data extract
  • Charts:
    • pie chart for distribution
    • bar chart for monthly trend
  • Metadata row:
    • generation timestamp
    • source table
    • filter range

8. After Connection: Customizing Flow

For customizing automation scripts:

  1. Start transaction (for example OVX4, OVXI, OVXB)
  2. Enter Change mode if needed
  3. Use New Entries / Copy As consistently
  4. Fill required fields only
  5. Save
  6. Handle transport popup:
    • assign existing request or create new one
  7. Read status bar and validate success

9. Transport Handling Pattern

During save, scripts should:

  1. Detect “Prompt for Customizing Request”
  2. If request field is empty, set existing request ID
  3. Confirm popup
  4. Close follow-up information popup
  5. Verify status = success (MessageType = S)

10. Validation Strategy

Always validate after script execution:

  • Re-open transaction and check entry exists
  • Cross-check table data in SE16N
  • Capture:
    • status type
    • status text
    • key fields created/updated
  • Export validation result if needed

 

Conclusion

By combining Codex for rapid script generation and SAP GUI Scripting for execution, teams can standardize repetitive SAP work: from reporting to customizing. The key to reliability is a strong connection layer, deterministic popup handling, and strict post-run validation.

entry.title: Re: Data Integration in ABAP Cloud: Data Exposure using SQL services
entry.content:
Hello @akuller 

Writing via SQL is mainly suitable for technical scenarios (imports, replication). Writing business logic via SQL would be architecturally possible, but RAP does not support it yet. Current recommendation is to write through business logic using OData.

Writable External Entities can execute Insert/Update/Delete operations in HANA databases. However RAP does not yet support this.  Besides, SQL Service cannot yet be used as a writable target due to missing SDA-level features. We shall wait for future releases to come!

Best regards

Safa

entry.title: How to add a check for requisitioner for purchase requisition created from plant maintenance orders?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We have a requirement to add a validation for the requisitioner field before creating Purchase requisition(s) from a Plant maintenance order. The Purchase requisition(s) could be created immediately from operation lines or external component lines while creating and saving the Plant maintenance order.

Requirement:

  • Restrict the saving of the PM order and the creation of the Purchase requisition if the validation fails with an error.
  • Return to the order creation/ change screen (IW31/ IW32) in case of error (error shouldn't terminate current session)
  • Allow the data in the PM order to be edited (error shouldn't block editing)
  • We need an enhancement that has all the necessary inputs for operation lines and components and allow to restrict the commit for saving the PM order and creation of the purchase requisition

What we have checked so far:

  • I tried to check the WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI using the method AT_SAVE, but this method lacks the information on the current operation lines and the components as inputs.
  • Then we tried adding the validating in BEFORE_UPDATE method of WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI, but this method gets called after the AT_SAVE method, so it doesn't serve the purpose.

If anyone has any experience with handling such validation, please share your inputs. Thanks in advance!

Regards,
Aavil

entry.title: Re: BOM Status with Change Number
entry.content:
Hello @nuno_pedroso1

Based on the screenshots, the production version itself appears to be maintained correctly. The consistency checks shows that both the task list exists and the BOM exists and the production version is valid from 19.02.2026. Also, your BOM with change number is valid from 02.02.2026, so from a pure master data perspective the setup looks consistent.

What I see in your screenshots is that you created the order with Scheduling Type 4 Current date. In standard SAP scheduling, this means the system uses the current date as the order start date. If the current date is earlier than 19.02.2026, the system may still reject the production version and issue C2142, even though the version appears in the selection list.

I recommend you create the production order with an order date that is explicitly on or after 19.02.2026 and then test the same production version again. If that works, then the issue is caused by the order key date rather than by Status 3 itself. If it still fails, then the setup shown in your screenshots would justify raising an SAP incident, because the BOM, task list and production version seem to appear all correct.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Prepare for refresh with /SDF/CALM_SYS_REFRESH
entry.content:
Good day All

We are planning a refresh of a DEV system and I need to export(backup) the Cloud ALM tables from the DEV system with report /SDF/CALM_SYS_REFRESH.

The report is mentioned in the house keeping section of below link

SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite 7 | SAP Help Portal

 

 

Doing the export with the report is not an issue but I am struggling to understand how to manage the transport of copies that the report creates.

The export must be done for the DEV system and the transport will obviously not be imported to QA and PRD as the tables are system specific. The report creates a backup of the tables which should be imported back into the DEV system after the refresh.

The transport of copies is created without a target system. When I try to release the transport I get a warning that I need to Specify a transport target.

If I edit the transport of copies to add a Target I add the DEV system details and get an error: Source client = target client not possible for transports.

I can still Save the Target details even though the error is displayed, but when I try to release the Transport of Copies, I get similar error as above.

I am not familiar with transport of copies and I guess when I release the ToC it shoud create the data and co files for the transport, which should be used to reimport the tables into the DEV system after the refresh.

Is there another way that the ToC should be released, something else that should be added as the Target or does "Export this request and save it" means something else?

Appreciate any guidance.

Werner

entry.title: Re: Transferring Cost Centers in a Service Organization
entry.content:
Hello @db11 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, the Profession Services projects is designed that a service organisation is linked to a central cost center, and once you have created a project, the project’s service organisation cannot be changed. Because of this, if you remove the cost center from the original service organisation while the project is still ongoing, the project can fail validation when you try to save changes, since the project still references that original service organisation.

Therefore, the standard approach for an organisational change is usually not to move the cost center away from the original service organisation for active projects. Instead, you should keep the old service organisation and its cost center assignment for the ongoing projects and create or use a new service organisation with its own cost center for newly created projects going forward. Doing this will help you avoid inconsistencies and align with SAP’s restriction that the service organisation of an existing project cannot be changed after you have created the project.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/f487a450daa94f5da93fb7049a125184.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/ccd0da55c2c4727fe10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US

I recommend you:

  • Check your ongoing project in the Professional Services Projects app
  • Keep your current service organisation to cost center assignment as long as the projects remains active
  • Create a new service organisation in Manage Service Organisations
  • Thereafter, assign the new cost center there and use the new service organisation only for new projects after the re-org date.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: End-to-End Mail Adapter Setup in SAP CPI Using Gmail (Step-by-Step Guide)
entry.content:
Introduction :  This article provides a comprehensive end-to-end guide to configuring the Mail Receiver Adapter in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) using a personal Gmail account. It explains the complete setup process, including the required Gmail configuration, security settings, and the detailed steps to configure the Mail Receiver Adapter within an integration flow. 

  • Enabling 2-Step Verification in Gmail

  • Generating a secure App Password

  • Creating User Credentials in CPI Security Material

Step 1 : Setting up the app password in Gmail.

To set up the app password 

  • Log in to your Gmail account.

  • Go to Google Account → Security.

  • Enable 2-Step Verification (mandatory).

  • After enabling 2-Step Verification, you will see App passwords.

  • Create a new App Password:

    • App: Mail

    • Device: Other (Custom name – SAP CPI)

  • Google will generate a 16-character password.

  • Use this password in CPI (without spaces).

Note : App Password works only if 2-Step Verification is enabled.

After creation you'll get a password which is generated 

Step 2 : Now we have  to store these credentials in cpi security material.

  • Go to Monitor → Manage Security → Security Material

  • Create User Credentials

  • Name: (e.g., gmailcredentials)

  • Username: Full Gmail address

  • Password: App Password (not Gmail login password)

The name which we have given here will be used in within the I flow

And the user should be  our mail ID  and in password we have give the app password which is generated.

Step 3 : Connectivity Test (certificate upload)

Now we have to test the connectivity towards the Gmail in Test connectivity tile.

  • Go to Monitor → Integrations & APIs → Connectivity Tests

  • Choose SMTP

  • Provide:

    • Host: smtp.gmail.com

    • Port: 587

    • Protection: STARTTLS

    • Authentication: Plain User/Password

    • Credential Name: gmailcredentials

Keystore: Upload the downloaded certificates here.

Step 4 : Create Integration Flow.

Here I have taken a simple content modifier with a body so that the message in content modifier will be triggered to our inbox

The body : 

 

Mail adapter : The To address should be same as what we have deployed in security material user credentials.

Step 5 : Testing 

In postman give the credential details and test it you'll getting the response and the mail will be triggered to your Gmail as well

We'll get a mail as below.

Summary : This blog explains how to configure and connect the SAP CPI Mail Adapter with a personal Gmail account. It covers the necessary Gmail security setup, including enabling 2-Step Verification and generating an App Password for secure authentication. The guide also walks through the step-by-step configuration of the Mail Receiver Adapter in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) and demonstrates how to send emails successfully using the  Gmail account configured.

entry.title: Attachment of Supporting Documents to Purchase Requisition (PR) – Item Level using DMS (CV01N)
entry.content:
The purpose of this document is to describe the process of attaching supporting documents (PDF/Word/Excel) to a Purchase Requisition (PR) at Item Level using SAP Document Management System (DMS).

This ensures:

  • Proper documentation for audit and compliance

  • Availability of technical specifications for approvers

  • Version control and traceability

  • Centralized document storage

Scope

This process applies to:

  • Purchase Requisitions with Item Level Release Strategy.

  • Documents such as technical specifications, quotations, drawings, approvals

  • Users with DMS authorization

Transaction Code Description

CV01NCreate Document Info Record
ME53NDisplay Purchase Requisition
ME52NChange Purchase Requisition

Prerequisites

  • PR must be created.

  • User must have DMS authorization.

  • Document Type for PR must be configured.

  • If required, PR item should be released before attachment.

    Procedure

    Step 1: Create Document Info Record

     

  1. Go to transaction CV01N.

  2. Enter:

    • Document Type (e.g., ZPR / DOC – as per configuration)

    • Description

    • Document Status

  3. Press Enter.

Note:

Document Status controls editability and release of the document.

Step 2: Maintain Object Links (Link PR Item)

  1. Go to Object Links tab.

  2. Enter:

    • Object Type → BANF

    • PR Number

    • PR Item Number

  3. Confirm the entries.

This links the document specifically to the PR Item.

 

Step 3: Upload Original File

  1. Go to Originals tab.

  2. Click Create.

  3. Select document type (PDF/Word/Excel).

  4. Enter description.

  5. Click on Original option.

  6. Select file from local system.

  7. Click ✔ (Tick).

After confirmation, the file appears in the Originals list

 

8. After, you are able to see the word document in the list of original tab. 

Step 4: Save Document

  1. Click Save.

  2. System generates Document Number (if internal numbering is configured).

  3. Document is now successfully linked to PR Item.

      4. Then Go to me53n t-code. 

Step 5: Verify Attachment in PR

  1. Go to transaction ME53N.

  2. Enter PR number.

  3. Select the relevant line item.

  4. Click on Document Button in item overview.

  5. Select document and click Display.

The attached file (PDF/Word) opens successfully.

 

Output:

Technical Information

     Object Type

  • PR Business Object: BUS2105

  • Object Link Type: BANF

    Important Tables

    Table Description
    DRAWDocument Header
    DRADObject Link
    DRAOOriginal Files
    SRGBTBRELAttachment relationships

    Workflow Relevance

    If PR release workflow is active:

    • Approvers can view attached DMS documents.

    • Attachments are accessible during release approval.

    • If document is not visible, check workflow container binding.

      Common Issues and Troubleshooting

      Issue Possible Cause Resolution
      Document not visible in ME53NObject link missingCheck DRAD table
      File not openingContent server issueCheck content repository configuration
      No authorizationMissing DMS rolesCheck SU53
      Document not linked to itemIncorrect item number enteredRecheck Object Link

      Advantages of Using DMS (CV01N)

      • Version Control

      • Centralized Document Storage

      • Change Management Support

      • Audit Compliance

      • Engineering Drawing Management

      • Classification Capability.

        Conclusion

        By using CV01N to attach documents at PR Item Level:

        • Documents are securely stored in DMS.

        • Linked properly using Object Type BANF.

        • Accessible during PR display and workflow approval.

        • Ensures compliance and document traceability.

entry.title: Re: Replicate the QR Code to custom Smartform, decoding f...
entry.content:
In a smartform you have two options for a QR code:

  1. system font: you pass the string value to the text node, set the appropriate paragraph / character type and done. Main problem: sizing and positioning can be extremely finicky
  2. embedded image: you can generate an image dynamically, you can even come up with a dynamic name and pass it to the graphic node but you cannot pass the image content itself to the graphic node! This works in Adobe Forms, not in SF.

What I did for a customer is: generate the image in the print program, save it to a dynamically-named image (so that concurrent printing of different invoices is possible), in the smartform read the appropriate name of the picture, when form is done remove the picture from DB.

 

*

entry.title: Implementing Bulk Upsert using Snowflake Adapter in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Overview 

In many integration projects, loading data into Snowflake is a common requirement. You may have used different approaches to insert or upsert data into Snowflake tables using Snowflake adapter depending on the system landscape and architecture : 

  • Insert operation in the adapter 
  • Executing SQL queries (Multi statements) 
  • Bulk Upsert Using External Stage (AWS S3, Azure Blob, or GCS) 

All these approaches work well. However, each may present itself with certain considerations.

For example: 

  • The Insert operation requires metadata configuration and may not be ideal for large datasets because constructing full INSERT statements can be overkilling while processing large datasets 
  • Executing queries means writing and managing SQL multi statements logic manually. 
  • External Stage requires hyperscaler storage (AWS, Azure, or GCS). 

Now let us assume a practical situation if the customer’s landscape does not include AWS, Azure, or GCS ?  

The latest version (1.2.0) of the adapter now also supports loading bulk data through  Snowflake Internal Stage. This is suggested for scenarios where user doesn’t have third-party storage services.

What’s New in the Snowflake Adapter?

The Snowflake Adapter now supports: 

  • File Staging operation (Put, List, Get) 
  • Bulk Upsert and Unload Operation with Access Type: Internal Stage 

With the above enhancements, you can: 

  • Upload, List and Fetch a file directly from Snowflake. 
  • Use that staged file to perform a bulk upsert into a target table. 
  • Unload the data from the Snowflake table to a stage location.

Let us first understand two important concepts before diving into the demo. 

What is File Staging in Snowflake? 

File staging in Snowflake is the process of uploading structured files (such as CSV or JSON) to a temporary storage location called a stage, from where Snowflake can efficiently load or merge the data into tables. 

Instead of inserting records one by one, data is processed in bulk using staged files, making it faster, scalable, and more suitable for large data loads. 

What is Snowflake Internal Stage? 

A Snowflake Internal Stage is a storage location managed entirely within Snowflake where files can be temporarily stored before being loaded into tables. 

It does not require AWS, Azure, or GCP storage and allows you to upload and process files directly inside Snowflake, making bulk data loading simpler and more streamlined. 

Integration Scenario : Bulk Upsert from Internal Stage  

Imagine a business scenario where a large volume of transactional records from an ERP system needs to be ingested into Snowflake for centralized storage, reporting, and analytics. 

  •  A straightforward approach would be to fetch the data from the ERP system and push it directly into Snowflake. However, when dealing with thousands or even lakhs of records, this method can take considerable time. 
  • Another option would be to write and execute custom SQL queries (such as Multi statements) to handle bulk processing. But maintaining large queries for high volumes of data is time-consuming and increases the risk of errors. 
  • Additionally, using an External Stage requires AWS, Azure, or GCP storage in the landscape. If the customer does not have a hyperscaler environment, that approach is simply not feasible. This is where the Internal Stage feature in the Snowflake Adapter becomes highly relevant.

In this blog, I will walk you through how to bulk insert records into Snowflake using the Snowflake Adapter in SAP Integration Suite. We will also briefly look at how the execution is handled internally within Snowflake for better understanding. 

Prerequisites

Before we begin, let us assume that: 

  • A target table with the required schema already exists in Snowflake. 
  • The connection between Snowflake and SAP Integration Suite is properly configured and working. 
  • Internal Stage is created in Snowflake. 

Let us now deep dive into the end-to-end implementation. 

Iflow Design in SAP CI 

For this example we have used a CSV file with 25k records in the Content Modifier which is uploaded to snowflake stage in the next step and then used for bulk upsert.

File Staging using Snowflake Adapter

The file is uploaded to the Snowflake Internal Stage using the PUT command. During configuration of the adapter, both the file name and the name of the Internal Stage must be specified. If required, a specific folder path within the stage can also be provided. Otherwise, the file will be uploaded to the default location of the Internal Stage. 
 

Bulk Upsert Using Snowflake Adapter

In this step, the data is upserted into the Snowflake table using the file that was previously staged in the Internal Stage. The configuration requires specifying the target table name and the Internal Stage name from which the data will be read. The file name should also be specified, as it determines which file’s data will be loaded into the table. If no file name is provided, the adapter will process all files available in the directory. Additionally, if the file is located within a specific subdirectory inside the stage, the corresponding path should be mentioned as well 
 
1. Also, note that Stages in Snowflake have a defined file format that determines which type of files can be uploaded and how those files are processed. Therefore, if you want to process file formats that are different from the one defined in the stage, you must provide the necessary parameters explicitly. 

For example, in the optional parameters field, you can specify: 

FILE_FORMAT = (TYPE = CSV) FORCE = TRUE ON_ERROR = CONTINUE

 2. Additionally, the File Name field can contain multiple files. For example: 

'BulkUpsert_Hundred_Rows1.csv','BulkUpsert_Hundred_Rows2.csv'

 3. Another important point to highlight is that if the file stored in the internal stage is compressed, the bulk upsert operation can automatically decompress the file and then load the data into the Snowflake table. 

For more details on optional parameters, please refer to the official Snowflake documentation: 
Optional parameters in Snowflake 

Now the iflow designing and configuration is complete, the iflow is ready to deploy. 

You can verify the same in the Snowflake application.
 
File is being staged in internal stage.  

 

File picked from stage and data upserted in snowflake table (EMP_DATA).  

Final Thoughts 

Using Internal Stage with the Snowflake Adapter makes bulk data loading into Snowflake simpler and more efficient, especially when external cloud storage is not part of the system landscape.

In our upcoming blog, we will also explore how to extract data from Snowflake tables via Snowflake Adapter. Stay tuned!

entry.title: SAP Asset Performance Management Meets SAP Situation Handling - Extended Framework
entry.content:
In this blog, I will outline how the SAP Situation Handling Extended Framework can be leveraged to notify Master Data Specialist when Maintenance Planner creates a maintenance objects such as Task Lists, Maintenance Plans, while implementing SAP Asset Performance Management Recommendations.

Business Value:

  • Operational Efficiency - Automating notifications eliminates manual follow-ups and reduces delays in master data validation.
  • Improved Collaboration and Transparency - This integration reduces communication gaps and ensures that master data is aligned with operational needs.
  • Optimized maintenance strategies extend asset life cycles and lower total cost of ownership.

If you want to gain a deep understanding of SAP Situation Handling - Standard Framework and SAP Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management, dive into my latest blog posts mentioned below.

  • S/4HANA Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) and Situation Handling Scenarios: Part-1 
  • S/4HANA Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) and Situation Handling Scenarios: Part-2  

let us have a look at the high level process flow of how SAP APM Recommendations integrate with SAP Situation Handling Extended Framework.

SAP APM Recommendations:

SAP Situation Handling Extended Framework:

Also, If you want to gain knowledge on SAP Signavio Process Manager & Process Collaboration Hub, please check out my blog post mentioned below. This blog provides a broad outline of process modelling.

  • The nuts and bolts of a well-thought-out SAP Signavio : Process Manager & Process Collaboration Hub 

My Business Use Case:

In my organization, maintenance planners operationalize the SAP APM Recommendations through maintenance task list and maintenance plans. The master data specialists team ensures governance by monitoring these processes through automated notifications enabled by Situation Handling Extended Framework.

Let us first focus on the key concepts of "Situation Handling - Extended Framework" and setup a situation type-extended for a maintenance object: Task List.

Situation Object: Technical model of a business object used in situation handling. This includes CDS views, navigation targets, events, and actions.

Situation Scenario: Business scenario that builds on situation objects. There are two kinds of situation scenarios. 

  • Object-based: Business scenario that builds on situation objects such as a Task List or a Maintenance Plan or a Maintenance Item.
  • Message-based: Business scenario that builds on system messages  from run types and reports.

As per my business use case, we will be exploring object-based situation scenarios listed below.

Situation Template: Template serves as a blueprint for creating a situation type.

Situation Type: Description of a business situation. For example, how a situation instance is triggered and who is informed about it.

Situation Instance: Instance of an exceptional issue which is displayed to the end user. It is generated according to the settings of the enabled situation type:

  • For Object-based situations: Whenever the conditions defined for the situation type are fulfilled.
  • For Message-based situations: Based on the run type settings for scheduled application jobs.

When situation instances occur, specified end users see the situations in the "My Situations-Extended" app, and also receive notifications on SAP FLP, Emails.

In order to get started with the Situation Handling Extended Framework, we must first define "Responsibility Management" for our object-based situation scenarios.

Intelligent systems need to determine agents(Responsible Persons) who are automatically notified about events and who are responsible for business processes and objects. 

The predefined settings in the situation template determine that the notification addresses only members of the "MAINT_MGMT" team category.

Team Information:

Responsibility Definitions and Member Function options allow us to select further filters  to determine the users responsible. For Instance, let us choose Main Work Center, Maintenance Planner Group, Maintenance Planning Plant, and Maintenance Plant.

Team Owner and Team Members:

Once we have enabled the Responsibility Team, we can proceed for  configuring the situation types-extended.

 

SAP provides the templates as listed below.

For demonstration purpose, I have used the EAM_MAINTENANCE_TASK_LIST template and created a Situation Type-Extended with an ID : Z_CAL_MAINT_TASK_LIST - Calcination Maintenance Task List.

we can provide the header information and situation instances criteria. However, the navigational actions are predefined with respect to each situation type.

The navigation actions are navigation options to business apps that can help users to solve a situation. In our business use case, we have "Find Maintenance Task List" app as a navigation action.

Now, let us define the conditions,

Mini Note: When we create a Task List via SAP APM Recommendation as part of Implement Recommendations, system creates a task list with status "1-Created" and assigns respective planning plant. So, let us consider the fields that SAP APM considers while creating a Task List.

and Batch job Scheduling parameters.

A situation Instance is created when the overall status of a maintenance task list is "Created"  and situation/notification is displayed as per the predefined text template.

Now let us assign the team that was created in the earlier step.

Now let us enable the Situation Type.

Now, let us perform FMEA(Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) assessment and create a "Task List" for one of the FMEA recommendations.

For Instance, I have performed an FMEA on Equipment 10001730 (Reactor 101 DIN2816 AIE). Based on the resulting RPN score, a recommendation was created/reviewed/released for Implementation.

FMEA Assessment:

Creating a Recommendation:

Let us now use "Manage Recommendations" app to update, review, and release the recommendation.

Review:

Ready for Release:

Released:

Once the recommendations are released, maintenance planners can initiate the implementation process.

Implement Recommendations:

As part of the recommendation implementation process, maintenance planners have to update the status of the recommendation to "Implementation In Process".

Once the status becomes "Implementation In Process", the maintenance planner can either assign the existing task list and maintenance plan or create a new task list and maintenance plan.

Since there is no task list exists in S/4HANA for this recommendation, let us create a new task list for this recommendation.

Now, as per the conditions maintained in the Situation Type "Z_CAL_MAINT_TASK_LIST", the system triggered the notifications.

Monitor Situations - Extended app helps us monitor situations created by the extended framework for situation handling. Monitoring Situation instances is enabled by default. We can display the activities performed for situation instances using different chart types and also as a table. To capture and show monitoring data, we must need to enable "Monitor Instances" while configuring the situation type-extended.

Let us first check the Monitor Situations - Extended app to visualize the situation instances that have been created so far.

Now, let us view the Notifications.

Option-1: We can view the notifications using "Bell Icon" on FLP screen.

Options-2: We can view notifications using "My Situations-Extended" app. Using this app, we can view all the situations as per the responsibility area. 

 

Scenario Selection Area: The list view shows all situations. We can go to situation page for further information or close the situation directly in the list view.

Situation Page: The situation page displays the detail information of a situation as of the point in time it was created, however, this can differ from the current date.

The option "Assign To Me" : We can assign a situation to ourselves. Once we assign to ourselves, then the system updates processor details with our name. If we stop working on this situation, we can unassign ourselves and the respective situation is displayed as "Open".

Now let us use the "Find Maintenance Task List" app to update the task list operations.

"Find Maintenance Task List" app will navigate us to the "Change Task List" app.

Let us now update the required operations for the task list group 36 and counter 1.

Once the respective Task List is updated, we can close the situation.

😊This brings me to the end of my process walk through on how we can leverage SAP Situation Handling Extended Framework for SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) - Implement Recommendations. I hope this blog proves useful to the Maintenance Management Team. I look forward to the adoption of the Situation Handling Extended Framework as part of SAP APM implementation to improve operational efficiency.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy. Chelikam

entry.title: Re: The quantity was copied as double
entry.content: The issue is not because of standard configurations but because of custom validation.  First check in vtaa at item level what routine is assigned against "Copying requirements".  It should have been a custom one specific to your client in which case, check with ABAP team by setting a Breakpoint to find out the root cause.

entry.title: How can we export SAP CPQ custom tables with more than a million records?
entry.content:
In SAP CPQ, the Custom Table Export functionality allows exporting table entries to Excel.

However, Excel worksheets have a maximum limit of 1,048,576 rows.

What is the recommended or officially supported approach for exporting custom tables that contain more than one million records?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How can we export SAP CPQ custom tables with more tha...
entry.content:
Excel files have a maximum limit of 1,048,576 rows per worksheet (Microsoft Excel limitation). Therefore, exporting more records than this limit into a single Excel file is not supported.

If a custom table contains more entries than this limit, the recommended approach is to export the data in batches by applying filters (for example, by ID ranges or other column filters) so that each export remains within Excel’s row limit. The exported files can then be combined if needed.

Alternatively, the ZIP export option may also be used, as it can be more suitable when exporting larger datasets.
